note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment aims to build Langmuir probes and emissive probes for measuring plasma potential in low-pressure, low-temperature plasmas. The goal is to understand the behavior of plasma under different conditions.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A person is shown assembling a probe by attaching wires to a cylindrical component.",
    "The assembly is then tested using a multimeter to measure electrical properties.",
    "The probe is inserted into a chamber equipped with various controls and measurements.",
    "Data from the experiment is displayed on a computer screen, showing graphs and numerical data."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Cylindrical components, wires, probes, multimeter, computer software for data analysis"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Multimeter, computer with data analysis software, control panel with various knobs and switches, cylindrical chamber with internal components"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Probe dimensions (1/8\" and 3/16\" diameter)",
    "Plasma parameters such as pressure (Pn), temperature (Tc), density (n_e), Debye length (λ_debye), and characteristic length (d_c)"
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "From assembling the probe to testing it with a multimeter",
    "From inserting the probe into the chamber to displaying data on a computer screen"
  ],
  "outcomes_or_indicators": [
    "Graphs showing plasma potential versus probe position",
    "Numerical data indicating plasma parameters at different pressures"
  ],
  "anything_unusual_or_notable": [
    "The use of a cylindrical component as part of the probe assembly",
    "The detailed measurement of plasma parameters and their relationship to probe position"
  ]
}
